content.claim: Portugal's 20-year experience shows that mass school closures in low-density regions (2005–2014) were followed by policy stabilization and shift toward targeted interventions and transport safeguards, not further consolidation.
content.context: Portugal's experience shows that school closure intensity from 2005–2014 did not automatically improve learning outcomes, and conditions of consolidation (travel burden, facility quality, community voice, pedagogical support) matter more for rural student access and equity than closure decisions alone.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
